item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the Tale is what really brought this piece together (it's what got me to rate up.)

As you read, you kind of feel sympathy for it and Sarah, and of course it being contained is a bit of a bummer. The tale, of course, reverses that feeling. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. It got emotion across, but it read… well, like someone who hasn't written before (or as the first post says, it IS your 3rd). Found myself skipping bits and pieces and getting detached from the article due to unprofessional sounding sections.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… anomalous Lilo and Stitch? That’s not a bad idea.

Also like the simple concept made better with backstory. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, of course you can! I never realized large sizes like that could have such an effect, my bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…so I just noticed that the image here is 1.88 MB. That's… unnecessary, to say the least.

Author, can I replace the image with a smaller version? Giant image files make mobile readers cry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I'm putting him all over my author page (because believe it or not this boi is my persona), so maybe look forward to that. If enough people like this though, maybe I'll make some sort of tale out of it, because peer pressure is a magical thing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reads like a good Series I article, and try as I might I just can't not upvote. It's clearly meant to be heartfelt and goofy, and I smiled throughout — that's mission accomplished, I'd say. Well done, and I hope we can see more of this good dumb orb boi someday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how this skip ended up. Good job, Bazzle!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed the mentionings of Sallie, that was meant to be Sarah. I keep writing her name like that for some reason without even noticing, I guess I’m still use to that being her name from the draft stages. Let me know if you spot any more Sallie’s in the article, it’d be really appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea, but uh… are Sally and Sarah 2 different people? If so, why is Sally mentioned exclusively outside the transcripts and Sarah mentioned exclusively inside of them?

The artifact listing layout is also fairly confusing with me having to scroll back and forth to determine which type of artifact is being interacted with in that portion of the article. There are also some light breaks in tone, which are forgivable in this case because they're fairly minor, but I can't upvote until it gets just a bit more spit and polish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My third SCP! Let's hope I can break my trend of low-rated pages with this one.

With this page, I hope to do something that isn't much done around here. Instead of having a really intriguing and complex concept with simple execution, I did a fairly simple concept with a (hopefully) intriguing and solid execution. I hope I accomplished my goal, or something similar.

Also yay, I'm an author now! (even if this skyrockets down, I'll be one for at most two days :p)

Image Credits:

https://www.flickr.com/photos/98640399@N08/9290203238

Thanks to all of the critiques that lead me through this mess in the draft stages, especially Captain Kirby, who helped me out numerous times through several major revisions, and helped me get this on track.
